name: Aaron Levie
summary: Argues that computer-use and on-the-fly code execution are foundational primitives for enterprise agents, with key challenges shifting to identity, permissions, and security controls.

name: Amjad Masad
summary: Highlighted a viral TSA wait-time app built on Replit as evidence that fast, practical consumer utilities are a proving ground for AI-native development platforms.

name: Kevin Weil
summary: Announced ChatGPT 26 for standout students and recent grads using frontier AI in science, including HQ access and $10K grants to accelerate early research momentum.

name: Alex Albert
summary: Shared a physicist report that Opus 4.5 operated around second-year grad level and enabled ~10x research acceleration, while signaling mobile-first AI workflows are becoming practical.

name: Thariq
summary: Said Anthropic acquired a computer-use company and shipped computer-use in about four weeks, highlighting compressed M&A-to-product integration cycles.

name: Nan Yu
summary: Shared that Codex caught an oblique feature-flag reference likely missed by many human reviewers, underscoring AI’s value in high-sensitivity code review.

name: Matt Turck
summary: Noted that tools like Claude Code may make the historical requirement of finding a technical cofounder before building increasingly obsolete.

name: Garry Tan
summary: Reported shipping a 2,400-line PR into YC’s 1.84M-line legacy codebase with AI coding workflows, arguing these methods transfer to large, debt-heavy production systems.

name: Sam Altman
summary: Stepped off Helion’s board as OpenAI and Helion explore larger collaboration, signaling governance tightening as AI-energy partnerships become more strategic.
